title: Hot Meals and Support for Dnipro Residents in Need
subtitle: Bringing warmth and dignity to those affected by war — one hot meal at a time

background.title: Background
paragraphs: Since late 2025, sustained Russian strikes on Ukraine's energy infrastructure have made large-scale power outages a daily occurrence in Ukrainian cities. While younger residents can seek out charging points or visit generator-powered shops and restaurants, the elderly — particularly those living in high-rise apartment buildings — face a near-total collapse of basic living conditions when the power goes out.

With elevators out of service, a single trip up and down the stairs each day pushes many to their physical limits. Electric stoves and water heaters become unusable, making cooking and heating a daily struggle. A large number of elderly residents live alone on modest pensions, with limited access to information and no means to afford eating out.
